page: Amazon Web Services c5d.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8124M processor with an x86_64 architecture, delivering 72 virtual CPUs across 36 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated CPU cores operate at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz and are supported by 144.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Local storage is provided via a bundled 1800 GB NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity features a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng and PassMark CPU scores in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also a key strength, with both cached small-block and uncached large-block read operations ranking in the top tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations show strong performance, while single-core tasks, software compilation, and static web serving fall within the average range. However, LLM text generation speeds represent a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% for both small and medium models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5d.18xlarge offers high compute density and fast local NVMe storage without the overhead of external network-attached storage. This hardware profile is highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads that require high-speed local scratch space, such as high-performance database operations, Redis caching layers, multi-threaded data decompression, and batch processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c5d.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by a 3.4 GHz Intel Xeon 8124M processor with 72 vCPUs and 36 physical cores. It features 144.0 GB of DDR4 memory and a bundled 1800 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, running on the Nitro hypervisor with 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong database and Redis SET operations. Conversely, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average to weak. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX-512 and AES instructions, backed by a 24 MB L3 cache. This configuration delivers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space, making it well-suited for high-throughput database operations, caching layers, and multi-threaded batch processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 72 vCPUs, 144 GB DDR4 RAM, and an 1800 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering strong multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5d.18xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 72 vCPUs, 144 GB RAM, and a bundled 1800 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: A compute-optimized platform featuring high-density virtual cores, fast DDR4 memory, and high-performance local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 72 vCPUs and 36 physical cores powered by a 3.4 GHz Intel Xeon processor
144.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong read and write bandwidth
Bundled 1800 GB local NVMe SSD instance store for high-speed scratch space
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and Redis SET benchmark performance
Designed for compute-intensive workloads, caching layers, and database operations
